My friend and I are planning a 2-day trip to London, followed by 3 days in Paris. Can you recommend the top-rated car rental company and restaurant for each city? We're also looking for the best-rated hotels in London and Paris. If there are multiple options, we prefer the most expensive ones. All prices should be in euros. Please provide the names of the hotels, car rental companies, and restaurants, as well as the total maximum expense for the 5-day trip (assuming 2 meals per day and that we take the most expensive rooms at the hotels, but we both stay in the same room). Thank you!
Sure! Let me start by fetching all the necessary information for both cities simultaneously!

## 🗺️ 5-Day London & Paris Trip Recommendations

| City | Hotel | Rating | Max Price/Night |

| 🇫🇷 Paris (3 nights) | **Luxury Palace** | ⭐ 5.0 | €1,000 |

> Both Paris hotels (Good Night & Luxury Palace) are rated 5.0 ⭐, but **Luxury Palace** is more expensive (up to €1,000/night vs €400/night), so it's the preferred choice.

---

### 🚗 Car Rentals

| City | Company | Rating | Price/Day |
|------|---------|--------|-----------|
| 🇬🇧 London (2 days) | **New Car Rental** | ⭐ 4.5 | €50 |
| 🇫🇷 Paris (3 days) | **Eiffel Tower Car Rental** | ⭐ 5.0 | €60 |

---

### 🍽️ Restaurants

| City | Restaurant | Rating | Price/Person |
|------|-----------|--------|--------------|
| 🇬🇧 London | **House of Sushi** | ⭐ 4.5 | €40 |
| 🇫🇷 Paris | **Le Baratin** | ⭐ 4.8 | €30 |

---

## 💶 Total Maximum Expense Breakdown

| Category | Calculation | Total |
|----------|-------------|-------|
| 🏨 London Luxury (2 nights) | €150 × 2 | €300 |
| 🏨 Luxury Palace (3 nights) | €1,000 × 3 | €3,000 |
| 🚗 New Car Rental – London (2 days) | €50 × 2 | €100 |
| 🚗 Eiffel Tower Car Rental – Paris (3 days) | €60 × 3 | €180 |
| 🍽️ House of Sushi – London (2 meals/day × 2 days × 2 people) | €40 × 4 × 2 | €320 |
| 🍽️ Le Baratin – Paris (2 meals/day × 3 days × 2 people) | €30 × 6 × 2 | €360 |
| **💰 Grand Total** | | **€4,260** |

---

> 📝 **Notes:**
> - Hotel prices are per room per night (you're both sharing one room).
> - Restaurant prices are per person, so multiplied by 2.
> - Car rental prices are per day, covering the full duration in each city.
> - All prices are in euros and reflect the **maximum** rates.
